Object.Name: Cabinet Controller Status
Object.Description1: The OMA Cabinet Controller Status shall provide electrical measurements related to street lines.

Item.Name: Total Current of all three phases
Item.Description: Sum of current measured on all three lines.

Item.Name: Overall Power Factor
Item.Description: Overall power factor.

Item.Name: Low power factor
Item.Description: Set to True when the overall power factor is below low power factor threshold.

Item.Name: Total active power
Item.Description: Total active power import on the 3 lines.

Item.Name: Total reactive power
Item.Description: Total reactive power import on the 3 lines.

Item.Name: Total apparent power
Item.Description: Total apparent power import on the 3 lines.

Item.Name: Total active energy
Item.Description: Active energy import (unified rate) on the 3 lines.

Item.Name: Total reactive energy
Item.Description: Reactive energy import, unified rate, on the 3 lines.

Item.Name: Frequency
Item.Description: Line frequency measured.

Item.Name: Averaging period
Item.Description: Seconds over which averaging themeasurements. If 0 or omitted means thatthe measurements are instantaneous.

Item.Name: Excessive load
Item.Description: True if an excessive load is detected, e.g when too much current is being imported

Item.Name: Unexpected load
Item.Description: True if an unexpected load is detected, e.g when current is imported despite the load being supposed to be disconnected

Item.Name: L1 Voltage
Item.Description: Instantaneous voltage measured between line 1 and neutral.

Item.Name: L2 Voltage
Item.Description: Instantaneous voltage measured between line 2 and neutral.

Item.Name: L3 Voltage
Item.Description: Instantaneous voltage measured between line 3 and neutral.

Item.Name: L1 Low voltage
Item.Description: Set to True when voltage 1 is below low voltage threshold.

Item.Name: L2 Low voltage
Item.Description: Set to True when voltage 2 is below low voltage threshold.

Item.Name: L3 Low voltage
Item.Description: Set to True when voltage 3 is below low voltage threshold.

Item.Name: L1 High voltage
Item.Description: Set to True when voltage 1 is above high voltage threshold.

Item.Name: L2 High voltage
Item.Description: Set to True when voltage 2 is above high voltage threshold.

Item.Name: L3 High voltage
Item.Description: Set to True when voltage 3 is above high voltage threshold.

Item.Name: L1 Current
Item.Description: Instantaneous current measured.

Item.Name: L2 Current
Item.Description: Instantaneous current measured.

Item.Name: L3 Current
Item.Description: Instantaneous current measured.

Item.Name: L1 Low current
Item.Description: Set to True when current 1 is below low current threshold.

Item.Name: L2 Low current
Item.Description: Set to True when current 2 is below low current threshold.

Item.Name: L3 Low current
Item.Description: Set to True when current 3 is below low current threshold.

Item.Name: L1 High current
Item.Description: Set to True when current 1 is above high current threshold.

Item.Name: L2 High current
Item.Description: Set to True when current 2 is above high current threshold.

Item.Name: L3 High current
Item.Description: Set to True when current 3 is above high current threshold.

Item.Name: L1 Unexpected load
Item.Description: True if an unexpected load is detected on line 1, e.g when current is consumed despite the load is supposed to be disconnected

Item.Name: L2 Unexpected load
Item.Description: True if an unexpected load is detected on line 2, e.g when current is consumed despite the load is supposed to be disconnected

Item.Name: L3 Unexpected load
Item.Description: True if an unexpected load is detected on line 3, e.g when current is consumed despite the load is supposed to be disconnected

Item.Name: Meter ID
Item.Description: Can be used to eventually associateseparate sets of measurements beingprovided with different averaging periods

Item.Name: Low voltage threshold
Item.Description: Set to True when voltage is below low voltage threshold.

Item.Name: High voltage threshold
Item.Description: Threshold above which the high voltage resource is set to True.

Item.Name: Low current threshold
Item.Description: Threshold below which the low current resource is set to True.

Item.Name: High current threshold
Item.Description: Threshold above which the high current resource is set to True.

Item.Name: Low power factor threshold
Item.Description: Threshold below which the low power factor resource is set to True.

Item.Name: Timestamp
Item.Description: The timestamp of when the measurement was performed.

Item.Name: Fractional Timestamp
Item.Description: Fractional part of the timestamp when sub-second precision is used (e.g., 0.23 for 230 ms).

Item.Name: Measurement Quality Indicator
Item.Description: Measurement quality indicator reported by a smart sensor. 0: UNCHECKED No quality checks were done because they do not exist or can not be applied. 1: REJECTED WITH CERTAINTY The measured value is invalid. 2: REJECTED WITH PROBABILITY The measured value is likely invalid. 3: ACCEPTED BUT SUSPICIOUS The measured value is likely OK. 4: ACCEPTED The measured value is OK. 5-15: Reserved for future extensions. 16-23: Vendor specific measurement quality.

Item.Name: Measurement Quality Level
Item.Description: Measurement quality level reported by a smart sensor. Quality level 100 means that the measurement has fully passed quality check algorithms. Smaller quality levels mean that quality has decreased and the measurement has only partially passed quality check algorithms. The smaller the quality level, the more caution should be used by the application when using the measurement. When the quality level is 0 it means that the measurement should certainly be rejected.
